props.ChoiceText: Soldiers crowded your doorway with urgent orders, and your craft began feeding war directly while deadlines ground away whatever pride was left in the work.
props.ChoiceBio: A military contract tied your workmanship to the machinery of war and to the strain of making violence in quantity.

0.TransitionText: Because a military contract tied your workmanship to the machinery of war, your life stayed with tools, labor, and the discipline of making things well.
0.TransitionPrompt: This could carry you into Craftsman, but you may remain on your current path instead.

1.TransitionText: Because a military contract tied your workmanship to the machinery of war, your work was claimed by war until you followed it into soldiering.
1.TransitionPrompt: This could carry you into Infantry, but you may remain on your current path instead.

2.TransitionText: Because a military contract tied your workmanship to the machinery of war, war and necessity dragged you away from the bench and into paid violence.
2.TransitionPrompt: This could carry you into Mercenary, but you may remain on your current path instead.

2.TargetText: Bulk war work hardened your shoulders, wrists, and hands into the shape demanded by hurried labor and military deadlines.
